titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William H. Genné Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by James E. Monsma and Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 12, 2020 at 23:36

did.abstract: The papers document Genné's religious work among college and university students. Material relating to Genné's workshops and published articles describe contemporary attitudes about marriage, family life and sexuality during the 1950s-1970s, and detail his work and influence in these fields. William Henry Genné (1910-1997) was educated at Bucknell and Yale Universities and was ordained a Baptist minister in 1934. Genné was active in student religious work in various colleges and universities in the U.S. during the period 1936-1951. From 1944 to 1947, he served as the Pacific Northwest Secretary, College and University Division, National Council of the YMCA. Genné went on from this early focus of his career to become an expert in the fields of marriage, sexuality and family ministries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William H. Genné Papers (RG 79), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical

chronitemset.event: William Henry Genné born in Philadelphia, PA
B.D., Yale Divinity School; ordained at First Baptist Church, Waterbury CT
Director of Student Religious Activities - Michigan State College
Married to Elizabeth Steel. Children: Nancy, 1941; William, 1943; Margaret, 1950; Susan, 1953.
Chaplain and Instructor in Sociology - Alfred University, Alfred, NY
Pacific Northwest Regional Secretary - College and University Division, National Council of YMCA
Teacher/Counsellor - The Clara Elizabeth Fund for Maternal and Family Health, Flint, Michigan
Director - Sexuality, Marriage and Family Ministries, National Council of Churches of Christ in the U.S.A.
Coordinator of Educational Services, Montclair Counseling Center

bioghist.p: For additional biographical information, lists of organizational affiliations, conferences attended, and works published, see Series IV, Box 30.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Following his graduation from Yale in 1936, Genné was active in student religious work. His files from this period, including those on the World Conference of Christian Youth in Amsterdam (1939) and the World Student Christian Federation Camp of 1939, complement other holdings of the Yale Divinity School Library in the area of religious work among college and university students. Genné went on from this early focus of his career to become an expert in the fields of marriage, sexuality and family ministries. In conjunction with his wife, Elizabeth Steel Genné, Genné conducted many workshops and wrote numerous articles for popular journals which described and influenced contemporary attitudes about marriage, sexuality and the family. His files document the issues and trends which dominated these fields, particularly during the 1950s through 1970s. The lists of conferences and organizations and subjects represented in Series III, Collected Material, indicate the scope of Genné's work.

The Correspondence of Series I deals primarily with arrangements for publications, conferences, workshops, and employment. In many cases, letters are filed under the name of an organization rather than an individual. Correspondence with R.H. Edwin Espy deals with the 1979 reunion of the 1939 Amsterdam World Conference of Christian Youth.

The Writings in Series II are those of William and Betty Genné. Genné frequently contributed articles to denominational magazines, other popular religious journals such as Christian Home, and to The Link , a periodical for soldiers published by the General Commission on Chaplains and Armed Forces Personnel. He also had articles on marriage, family life, and sexuality published in secular journals such as True Story and Redbook . The sermons and addresses in Series II stem primarily from Genné's years as a college chaplain at Pacific University and Alfred University.

The Collected Material of Series III documents the conferences, organizations, and topics that were the focal points for Genné's career. The chronologically arranged conferences range from international gatherings to Sunday school workshops. The May 1977 workshop at Trinity United Methodist Church entitled "New Life for Me and My Family" is a typical example of the Gennés' workshops on family and marriage enrichment. Of particular interest in the material arranged by organization are the files documenting the National Council of Churches' Family Ministries department. Important publications of the National Council of Churches include a study guide on abortion, collected denominational statements on sexuality, and resource guides on sexuality and sex education. The collected material arranged by subject comes from a broad range of sources such as magazine articles, government pamphlets and denominational publications. Documented most extensively are the topics of marriage/family enrichment and sexuality/sex education.

The Biographical Documentation of Series IV is biographical in a broadly defined sense. Files from different periods in Genné's life include material ranging from student papers to correspondence with the Selective Service System, reports to his employers, resumes, and documentation of television appearances. Three folders of articles about Genné provide concise information about his life and work.

The Audio-visual Material in Series V includes largely unidentified photographs related to Genné's activities as well as tapes, films and visual teaching aids used in presentations on marriage enrichment and sexuality.

corpname.part: National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The correspondence is arranged alphabetically by the name of the sender or recipient other than Genné. In most cases, letters are filed according to an organizational rather than a personal name. Full names are listed only for substantive files.

did.unittitle: Clara Elizabeth Fund for Maternal Health

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged by format and chronologically within most formats.

did.unittitle: Books and pamphlets by Genné and others
Reviews and Advertisements of Husbands and Pregnancy

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this series is divided into three categories:

did.unittitle: Baptist Young People's Union of America
Pastoral Conference on Family Life Education
North American Conference on Church and Family
Symposium on U.S. Population and Parenthood
Riverside Church Series on Sex and Christian Responsibility
Fifth National Conference on Family Life of the Methodist Church
Marriage and Pre-Marriage Workshop for Clergy
Flint, Michigan YWCA and Community Schools
Perspectives on Sexuality, Sex Education and Mental Health
Pascack Ecumenical School Workshop on Marriage Potential
SIECUS Conference on Sex, Love and Intimacy - Whose Life Styles?
First Congregational Church, Verona, New Jersey: Series on Sexuality and Values
American Baptist Men and American Baptist Women
Chatham United Methodist Church - Christian Families in Today's World
Cave Spring United Methodist Church - What Makes a Family Christian?
Presbyterian Meeting House, Alexandria, VA: Lenten Series
Conference on Human Issues in Management
First Presbyterian Church, Greenwood, SC: To Love is to Grow
Changing Family Styles: The Church's Response
Western Washington State College: Workshop on Sexuality and Human Relationships
Texas Council of Churches General Assembly
Ridgewood United Methodist Church: Making the Most of the Middle Years
New Jersey Medical School Seminar in Human Sexuality
Southern Methodist Univerity: Images for the Future
Greece Baptist Church: Family Emphasis Weekend
Connecticut Conference of U.C.C.: Spring Planting
Trinity United Methodist Church: New Life for Me and My Family
North Shore Unitarian Society: Marriage Encounter Weekend
Warwick Conference Center: Marriage Enrichment Weekend
New Jersey Nursing Students Annual Convention
"Roots and Wings": Celebrating Stability and Change in Families
Cleveland Heights Christian Church: Family Enrichment Seminar
Heidelberg College: Clergy for Heidelberg Day
New Brunswick Presbyterian Church: Helping Build Families Today
Church Women United in New York State: Annual Meeting
Montclair State College: Sex Education Conference
Madison Methodist Church: Christian Families in Today's World
Lawrence Road Presbyterian Church: To Love is to Grow
John Knox Presbyterian Church: Relativity Celebration
Bound Brook Presbyterian Church: Building Christian Families
Community Church of Mountain Lakes: Christian Life Styles in a Contemporary World
National Consultation on the Filipino Family
Pacific Council of Churches: Family Life Seminar
Schools and Challenges of Family Education
Presbytery of New Brunswick: Festival of Learning
United Church Board for Homeland Ministries: Family Life Conference
Schenectady YWCA: Human Sexuality: A Family Affair
United Church of Christ: Family Life
Association of Couples for Marriage Enrichment Center for Parish Development
Association of Couples for Marriage Enrichment Center for Parish Development - Newsletter
Christian Youth Building a New World
Church Women United Family Life Advisory Committee
National Assembly of Student Christian Associations
Sex Information and Education Council of the U.S.
United Church of Christ Family Life Priority Working Group

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this series is arranged in a generally chronological sequence with folder headings listed to indicate the nature of each file.

did.unittitle: Clara Elizabeth Fund for Maternal Health

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this series is arranged by format and chronologically within each format.

did.unittitle: Records and Books - "The Marriage Enrichment Program"
